note: Write-parity (create/copy/delete) DEFERRED pending CRF regulatory sign-off (CLAUDE Rule 5). No PHI committed: patients referenced by numeric code; matched names compared via md5, never stored.

scenarios.name: list row fidelity
scenarios.assert: each row unidade/numero/data/paciente/prescritor == sau_recesp; pacienteNome == sys_pes.PesNom (md5)

scenarios.name: filter by nome
scenarios.assert: totalElements == count WHERE PesNom ILIKE %token% via SAU_PAC->SYS_PES

scenarios.name: detail field-by-field (master + itens)
scenarios.assert: all master fields == sau_recesp row; itens[] (11 fields) == sau_recesp1 rows in seq order (text via md5)

scenarios.name: derivations
scenarios.assert: pacienteIdade == age(RecEspDat,PesNasDat) [57/46/55]; pacienteNomeExibicao==PesNom (no social name in snapshot); avisos==[] (CNS present)
scenarios.coverage_note: R13/R16 positive branches: 0 snapshot prescriptions have blank CNS or social name -> covered by unit/IT, not parity

scenarios.name: ordering
scenarios.assert: ORDER BY recespunicod ASC, recespcod DESC

deferred_write_scenarios: create valid (server-allocated numero = max+1 per unit, R1)
delete (legacy hard-delete R29; modern blocks 409 — intended improvement, OQ2)
verdict: PARITY 9/9 (read surface). Write-parity deferred pending CRF sign-off; not cleared for cutover.
